root_text.sn46.38:1.1: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye ariyasāvako aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbaṁ cetaso samannāharitvā ohitasoto dhammaṁ suṇāti, imassa pañca nīvaraṇā tasmiṁ samaye na honti. Satta bojjhaṅgā tasmiṁ samaye bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchanti.
root_text.sn46.38:2.1: Katame pañca nīvaraṇā tasmiṁ samaye na honti?
root_text.sn46.38:2.2: Kāmacchandanīvaraṇaṁ tasmiṁ samaye na hoti, byāpādanīvaraṇaṁ tasmiṁ samaye na hoti, thinamiddhanīvaraṇaṁ tasmiṁ samaye na hoti, uddhaccakukkuccanīvaraṇaṁ tasmiṁ samaye na hoti, vicikicchānīvaraṇaṁ tasmiṁ samaye na hoti.
root_text.sn46.38:2.3: Imassa pañca nīvaraṇā tasmiṁ samaye na honti.
root_text.sn46.38:3.1: Katame satta bojjhaṅgā tasmiṁ samaye bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchanti?
root_text.sn46.38:3.2: Satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati …pe… upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn46.38:3.3: Ime satta bojjhaṅgā tasmiṁ samaye bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchanti.
root_text.sn46.38:3.4: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye ariyasāvako aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbaṁ cetaso samannāharitvā ohitasoto dhammaṁ suṇāti, imassa pañca nīvaraṇā tasmiṁ samaye na honti. Ime satta bojjhaṅgā tasmiṁ samaye bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchantī”ti.

translation_text.sn46.38:1.1: “Mendicants, sometimes a noble disciple pays attention, applies the mind, concentrates wholeheartedly, and actively listens to the teaching. At such a time the five hindrances are absent, and the seven awakening factors are fully developed.
translation_text.sn46.38:2.1: What are the five hindrances that are absent?
translation_text.sn46.38:2.2: Sensual desire, ill will, dullness and drowsiness, restlessness and remorse, and doubt.
translation_text.sn46.38:2.3: These are the five hindrances that are absent.
translation_text.sn46.38:3.1: And what are the seven awakening factors that are fully developed?
translation_text.sn46.38:3.2: The awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion, and equanimity.
translation_text.sn46.38:3.3: These are the seven awakening factors that are fully developed.
translation_text.sn46.38:3.4: Sometimes a noble disciple pays attention, applies the mind, concentrates wholeheartedly, and actively listens to the teaching. At such a time the five hindrances are absent, and the seven awakening factors are fully developed.”
